description:
You are a data-scientist who has received a mixed Apache access log that contains
    (a) malformed records, (b) records that reveal personally-identifiable information
    (PII) such as e-mail addresses or North-American phone numbers, and
    (c) records with syntactically invalid IPv4 addresses.
    Your objective is to perform a *regex-based* cleansing of this file and to
    generate both a filtered log and an audit log summarising what was removed.

    STARTING MATERIAL
    1. Directory:  /home/user/datasets/log_cleanup
    2. File:       /home/user/datasets/log_cleanup/raw_web_events.log  
       – 15 text lines already exist in this file (see the file, do **NOT**
         hard-code assumptions; always read from disk).

    REQUIRED END-STATE
    Inside /home/user/datasets/log_cleanup **after you finish** there must be *exactly* the
    following additional two files with the precise names and formats shown:

    ------------------------------------------------------------------------
    A) /home/user/datasets/log_cleanup/cleaned_web_events.log
       • Contains only the “good” log lines.
       • The **only** lines that are considered “good” and must be kept are those
         that simultaneously satisfy *all* four conditions below:
           1. The line begins with a syntactically valid IPv4 address
              (four octets, each 0-255, no leading zeros required).
           2. The HTTP method appearing in the quoted request is either GET or POST
              (case sensitive).
           3. The line does NOT contain an e-mail address
              (pattern: something@something).
           4. The line does NOT contain a North-American phone number written as
              3 digits, a dash, 3 digits, a dash, 4 digits
              (e.g. 555-123-4567).
       • Preserve the **original ordering** of the kept lines.
       • Do **not** add blank lines or a trailing newline beyond what already
         exists for each kept record.

    ------------------------------------------------------------------------
    B) /home/user/datasets/log_cleanup/processing.log
       • This is a tiny YAML-like, *strictly 8-line* audit file written in the
         exact key: value format shown below (no extra spaces before keys, a
         single space after every colon, UNIX newlines):

           total: <integer>
           removed: <integer>
           kept: <integer>
           breakdown:
             contains_email: <integer>
             contains_phone: <integer>
             malformed_ip: <integer>
             malformed_format: <integer>

         Explanation of keys:
           total            – total number of lines found in raw_web_events.log
           removed          – total discarded lines
           kept             – total kept lines (must be total minus removed)
           contains_email   – lines discarded ONLY because they contained email
           contains_phone   – lines discarded ONLY because they contained phone
           malformed_ip     – lines discarded ONLY because of an invalid IPv4
           malformed_format – lines discarded because they were not recognisable
                              Apache log lines at all
       • The automated tests will check that the arithmetic
         total = removed + kept
         and that the four breakdown numbers sum to removed.

    ------------------------------------------------------------------------
    IMPLEMENTATION REQUIREMENTS & HINTS
    • Use one or more shell utilities (grep, awk, sed, etc.) with regular
      expressions to do the filtering; do not manually re-type the good lines.
    • You are free to create temporary helper files but they must reside in the
      same directory and should be removed before finishing.
    • Do **NOT** move or rename raw_web_events.log.

    VERIFICATION
    The grading script will verify:
      1. Both target files exist at the expected paths.
      2. cleaned_web_events.log contains *only* the correct lines and in the
         original order.
      3. processing.log is exactly eight lines, matches the required key order,
         and all integer values are correct and consistent with the contents of
         both raw_web_events.log and cleaned_web_events.log.

    Produce no additional output files beyond the two defined above.
truth:
<!--
      Ground-truth data used by the autograder.
      The agent MUST NOT see anything inside this block.
    -->

    <!-- 1. Expected content of raw file (already present when container is launched) -->

    <!-- 2. Expected content of cleaned file after agent finishes -->

    <!-- 3. Expected content of processing log -->

  contains_phone: 1
  malformed_ip: 1
  malformed_format: 2
